     8→        "content": "     1→# Git Provider Service CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→Provides GitLab REST API integration for automated merge request creation and management in transflow workflows.
     5→
     6→## Narrative Summary
     7→The git provider service implements a clean interface for interacting with Git forge providers (currently GitLab, extensible to GitHub). It handles authentication, project URL parsing, merge request lifecycle management, and error handling. The service supports both creating new merge requests and updating existing ones based on source branch matching.
     8→
     9→The provider follows the principle of graceful degradation - MR creation failures do not break the parent workflow, allowing transformations to complete even when Git forge integration is unavailable.
    10→
    11→## Key Files
    12→- `interface.go:5-26` - GitProvider interface and data structures
    13→- `gitlab.go:14-272` - GitLab REST API implementation
    14→- `gitlab.go:45-80` - Main CreateOrUpdateMR orchestration logic
    15→- `gitlab.go:82-117` - Existing MR detection and conflict resolution
    16→- `gitlab.go:218-250` - GitLab project URL parsing and validation
    17→- `gitlab_test.go:1-150` - Mock implementation for testing
    18→
    19→## API Interface
    20→### GitProvider Interface
    21→- `CreateOrUpdateMR(ctx, config) -> (*MRResult, error)` - Primary MR management
    22→- `ValidateConfiguration() -> error` - Pre-flight configuration check
    23→
    24→### Data Structures
    25→- `MRConfig:12-19` - Input parameters for MR creation
    26→- `MRResult:22-26` - Output result with URLs and metadata
    27→
    28→## Integration Points
    29→### Consumes
    30→- GitLab REST API: Projects, merge requests endpoints
    31→- Environment variables: GITLAB_URL, GITLAB_TOKEN
    32→
    33→### Provides
    34→- Merge request creation/updates for transflow workflows
    35→- Project URL validation and parsing
    36→- Authentication validation
    37→
    38→## Configuration
    39→Required environment variables:
    40→- `GITLAB_TOKEN` - GitLab API token with project access
    41→- `GITLAB_URL` - GitLab instance URL (optional, defaults to https://gitlab.com)
    42→
    43→Supported repository URL formats:
    44→- `https://gitlab.example.com/namespace/project.git`
    45→- `https://gitlab.com/group/subgroup/project.git`
    46→
    47→## Authentication Patterns
    48→- Bearer token authentication via Authorization header
    49→- Token validation on service initialization
    50→- Graceful error handling for invalid/expired tokens
    51→
    52→## Key Patterns
    53→- Interface-based design for provider extensibility
    54→- URL parsing with validation (see gitlab.go:218-250)
    55→- Idempotent operations - safe to retry MR creation
    56→- Context-aware HTTP requests with timeout support
    57→
    58→## Related Documentation
    59→- `../../cli/transflow/CLAUDE.md` - Main consumer service
    60→- GitLab REST API documentation for merge requests endpoint

     2→task: h-implement-job-submission-infrastructure
     3→branch: feature/implement-job-submission-infrastructure
     4→status: completed
     5→created: 2025-01-09

     7→modules: [internal/cli/transflow, internal/orchestration, api/arf]
     8→---
     9→
    10→# Job Submission Infrastructure for Transflow MVP
    11→
    12→## Problem/Goal
    13→
    14→Implement the critical job submission and monitoring infrastructure required for transflow's LangGraph healing workflow. This is the foundational piece that enables:
    15→- Planner job submission (LangGraph analysis of build failures)
    16→- Parallel healing branch execution (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)
    17→- Reducer job coordination (first-success-wins logic)
    18→- Job status monitoring and artifact collection
    19→
    20→Without this infrastructure, the healing workflow cannot function and the MVP cannot be completed.
    21→
    22→## Success Criteria
    23→
    24→- [x] Implement `SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` for batch job submission and monitoring
    25→- [x] Create orchestration helpers for planner/reducer/healing job types  
    26→- [x] Add job status polling with proper timeout handling
    27→- [x] Implement artifact collection from completed jobs
    28→- [x] Write comprehensive unit tests for all job submission logic
    29→- [x] Integration test with mock Nomad to validate workflow
    30→- [x] Update `TransflowRunner` to use job submission for healing cycle
    31→- [x] Document usage patterns and error handling
    32→- [x] Pass all existing tests + new test coverage > 80%
    33→- [x] Build compiles successfully (`go build ./...`)
    34→- [x] Update CHANGELOG.md with implementation details
    35→
    36→## Context Files
    37→
    38→<!-- Core requirements from roadmap -->
    39→- @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md                           # MVP specification and requirements
    40→- @roadmap/transflow/orchestrator_submit_wait_terminal.md  # Job submission guidance
    41→- @roadmap/transflow/orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md        # Parallel execution pattern
    42→- @roadmap/transflow/jobs/planner.hcl                    # Planner job template
    43→- @roadmap/transflow/jobs/reducer.hcl                    # Reducer job template
    44→- @roadmap/transflow/jobs/llm_exec.hcl                   # LLM execution job template
    45→
    46→<!-- Current implementation -->
    47→- @internal/cli/transflow/runner.go                   # Main runner that needs job integration
    48→- @internal/cli/transflow/run.go                      # CLI entry point with job flags
    49→- @internal/orchestration/                            # Existing orchestration package
    50→- @api/arf/multi_repo_orchestrator.go               # Reference for job patterns
    51→
    52→<!-- Test patterns -->
    53→- @internal/cli/transflow/runner_test.go             # Existing test structure
    54→- @AGENTS.md                                         # TDD requirements and testing guidelines
    55→
    56→## User Notes
    57→
    58→**Critical MVP Blocker**: This task represents the most critical missing piece for transflow MVP. All healing functionality depends on reliable job submission and monitoring.
    59→
    60→**TDD Approach Required**: Follow @AGENTS.md mandatory TDD cycle:
    61→1. RED: Write failing tests for job submission scenarios
    62→2. GREEN: Implement minimal working job submission logic  
    63→3. REFACTOR: Test integration on VPS with real Nomad
    64→
    65→**Architecture Constraints**:
    66→- Must integrate with existing `internal/orchestration` package
    67→- Follow established patterns from ARF multi-repo orchestrator
    68→- Support both synchronous (wait for completion) and asynchronous job execution
    69→- Handle job timeouts, failures, and cancellation gracefully
    70→
    71→**Reference Implementation**: Use `orchestrator_submit_wait_terminal.md` and `orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md` as design guidance - these contain the architectural patterns already validated for the platform.
    72→
    73→## Context Manifest
    74→
    75→### How This Currently Works: Transflow Orchestration Architecture
    76→
    77→When a user initiates a transflow run via `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`, the request flows through a sophisticated multi-layer architecture that's designed around healing workflows and job orchestration.
    78→
    79→**Current Entry Point and Configuration Flow:**
    80→The command enters through `internal/cli/transflow/run.go::runTransflow()`, which parses flags and loads the YAML configuration via `internal/cli/transflow/config.go::LoadConfig()`. The configuration system supports:
    81→- Basic workflow definition (id, target_repo, base_ref, steps)
    82→- Lane-specific deployment configuration (lane A-G auto-detection)
    83→- Build timeout configuration with validation
    84→- Self-healing configuration (max_retries, cooldown, enabled flag)
    85→
    86→**Current Implementation Status:**
    87→The transflow system is **partially implemented** with several critical gaps:
    88→- ✅ OpenRewrite recipe execution via ARF integration (`internal/cli/transflow/integrations.go`)
    89→- ✅ Git operations wrapper around `api/arf/git_operations.go`
    90→- ✅ Build checking via `SharedPush` from `internal/cli/common/deploy.go`  
    91→- ✅ Basic runner structure with dependency injection (`internal/cli/transflow/runner.go`)
    92→- ❌ **JOB SUBMISSION AND MONITORING INFRASTRUCTURE** (this task's focus)
    93→- ❌ Healing workflow orchestration (planner → fanout → reducer)
    94→- ❌ Artifact collection from completed jobs
    95→
    96→**Existing Orchestration Infrastructure:**
    97→The system leverages `internal/orchestration/` package which provides:
    98→
    99→1. **Submit Functions** (`internal/orchestration/submit.go`):
   100→   - `Submit(jobPath)` - Basic HCL job registration
   101→   - `SubmitAndWaitHealthy()` - For services (not applicable to batch jobs)
   102→   - **`SubmitAndWaitTerminal()`** - **ALREADY EXISTS** for batch jobs but needs integration
   103→   - `ValidateJob()` and `PlanJob()` utilities
   104→
   105→2. **Monitoring Infrastructure** (`internal/orchestration/monitor.go`):
   106→   - `HealthMonitor` with SDK adapter pattern
   107→   - Job status polling with allocation state tracking
   108→   - Terminal state detection (complete/failed) 
   109→   - Endpoint discovery for running services
   110→
   111→3. **Nomad SDK Integration** (`internal/orchestration/monitor_sdk_adapter.go`):
   112→   - Clean abstraction over Nomad API client
   113→   - Environment-driven configuration (`NOMAD_ADDR`)
   114→   - Allocation status parsing with deployment health checks
   115→
   116→**Current Runner Architecture:**
   117→The `TransflowRunner` in `internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` follows dependency injection pattern:
   118→- Interface-based design (`GitOperationsInterface`, `RecipeExecutorInterface`, `BuildCheckerInterface`)
   119→- Factory pattern via `TransflowIntegrations` in `integrations.go`
   120→- Comprehensive test coverage with mock implementations in `runner_test.go`
   121→
   122→**Existing Job Templates and Patterns:**
   123→The healing workflow architecture is defined through HCL job templates in `roadmap/transflow/jobs/`:
   124→
   125→1. **Planner Job** (`planner.hcl`):
   126→   - LangGraph-based Docker container execution
   127→   - Input: `inputs.json` with language, lane, last_error, deps
   128→   - Output: `plan.json` with parallel healing options
   129→   - Environment variables: MODEL, TOOLS_JSON, LIMITS_JSON, RUN_ID
   130→   - Host volume mounts for context, kb (knowledge base), and output
   131→
   132→2. **Reducer Job** (`reducer.hcl`):  
   133→   - Processes branch results and determines next actions
   134→   - Input: `history.json` with plan_id, branches, winner
   135→   - Output: `next.json` with action and notes
   136→   - Lighter resource requirements (300 CPU, 512 MB memory)
   137→
   138→3. **Healing Branch Jobs**:
   139→   - **LLM Exec** (`llm_exec.hcl`): Direct LLM-generated patch application
   140→   - **ORW Apply** (`orw_apply.hcl`): OpenRewrite recipe execution
   141→
   142→All job templates use:
   143→- Batch type with restart attempts = 0, mode = "fail"
   144→- Host volume mounts for workspace isolation
   145→- Environment variable placeholders for runtime substitution
   146→- Consistent timeout patterns (5m kill_timeout, job-specific execution timeout)
   147→
   148→### For Job Submission Implementation: What Needs to Connect
   149→
   150→Since we're implementing the job submission infrastructure that enables healing workflows, this connects to the existing architecture at several critical integration points:
   151→
   152→**The TransflowRunner Integration Point:**
   153→After a build failure in `runner.go::Run()`, the healing cycle needs to be triggered. Currently, the runner stops execution on build failure (lines 381-398), but the MVP requires planner job submission at this point. The architecture calls for:
   154→
   155→1. **Planner Submission** after build failure detection
   156→2. **Plan Parsing** to extract healing options 
   157→3. **Fanout Execution** of parallel healing branches (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)
   158→4. **First-Success-Wins** cancellation logic
   159→5. **Reducer Job** to determine final actions
   160→
   161→**Command-Line Interface Extensions:**
   162→The `run.go` already has partial job submission flags:
   163→- `--plan` flag renders and optionally submits planner job
   164→- `--exec-llm-first` and `--exec-orw-first` for branch execution
   165→- `--apply-first` for diff application and validation
   166→- Environment-driven submission control via `TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT=1`
   167→
   168→**ARF System Integration Patterns:**
   169→The existing `api/arf/openrewrite_dispatcher.go` demonstrates the job submission pattern we need to follow:
   170→- Nomad client initialization with environment configuration
   171→- Job template rendering with placeholder substitution  
   172→- Error handling and retry logic
   173→- Infrastructure validation before job submission
   174→
   175→**Volume and Artifact Management:**
   176→The job templates expect host volume sources:
   177→- `transflow-context` - Input data for jobs
   178→- `transflow-kb` - Knowledge base for planner
   179→- `transflow-out` - Artifact collection from completed jobs
   180→- `transflow-history` - Branch execution results for reducer
   181→
   182→**Environment Variable Patterns:**
   183→Jobs expect consistent environment configuration:
   184→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL` (default: "gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06")
   185→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` (JSON allowlisting tools)  
   186→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` (JSON with max_steps, max_tool_calls, timeout)
   187→- Recipe-specific vars for ORW jobs (RECIPE_CLASS, RECIPE_COORDS)
   188→
   189→**Current Integration Gaps:**
   190→The existing `SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` function provides the core job submission capability, but we need:
   191→1. **Helper functions** for each job type (planner, reducer, llm-exec, orw-apply)
   192→2. **Asset rendering** functions (already partially implemented in `runner.go`)
   193→3. **Fanout orchestration** following the pseudo-code in `orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md`
   194→4. **Artifact collection** after job completion
   195→5. **Integration with TransflowRunner** healing cycle
   196→
   197→### Technical Reference Details
   198→
   199→#### Core Functions to Implement
   200→
   201→**Job Submission Helpers:**
   202→```go
   203→// In internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go
   204→func SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext) (*PlannerResult, error)
   205→func SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, history *BranchHistory) (*ReducerResult, error) 
   206→func SubmitLLMExecJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, option BranchSpec) (*BranchResult, error)
   207→func SubmitORWApplyJob(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, option BranchSpec) (*BranchResult, error)
   208→```
   209→
   210→**Fanout Orchestration:**
   211→```go
   212→// Following orchestrator_fanout_sketch.md pattern
   213→func RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, plan []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (winner BranchResult, results []BranchResult, err error)
   214→func executeBranch(ctx context.Context, run *RunContext, spec BranchSpec, cancelCh <-chan struct{}) BranchResult
   215→```
   216→
   217→#### Data Structures
   218→
   219→**Job Execution Context:**
   220→```go
   221→type RunContext struct {
   222→    Config       *TransflowConfig
   223→    WorkspaceDir string
   224→    RepoPath     string
   225→    OutDir       string
   226→    JobTimeout   time.Duration
   227→    BuildTimeout time.Duration
   228→    Allowlist    []string
   229→}
   230→```
   231→
   232→**Branch Specifications (from fanout sketch):**
   233→```go
   234→type BranchSpec struct {
   235→    ID     string
   236→    Type   string // "human" | "llm-exec" | "orw-gen" 
   237→    Inputs map[string]any
   238→}
   239→
   240→type BranchResult struct {
   241→    ID         string
   242→    Status     string // success|failed|canceled|timeout
   243→    Artifact   string // diff.patch path
   244→    JobName    string  
   245→    JobID      string
   246→    StartedAt  time.Time
   247→    FinishedAt time.Time
   248→    Notes      string
   249→}
   250→```
   251→
   252→#### Configuration Requirements
   253→
   254→**Environment Variables:**
   255→- `NOMAD_ADDR` - Nomad cluster address (orchestration package)
   256→- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL` - LLM model specification
   257→- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` - Tool allowlist JSON
   258→- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` - Execution limits JSON
   259→- `TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT` - Enable actual job submission (vs dry-run)
   260→
   261→**Validation Schema (existing in transflow/schema.go):**
   262→- `validatePlanJSON()` - Validates planner output structure
   263→- `validateNextJSON()` - Validates reducer output structure
   264→
   265→#### File Locations
   266→
   267→**Implementation Structure:**
   268→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go` - Core job submission helpers
   269→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestration.go` - Healing workflow orchestration
   270→- Extension: `internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` - Integrate healing cycle into main Run()
   271→- Extension: `internal/orchestration/submit.go` - Add job-specific utilities if needed
   272→
   273→**Test Implementation:**
   274→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/job_submission_test.go` - Unit tests for submission logic
   275→- New file: `internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestration_test.go` - Integration tests for healing flow
   276→- Extend: `internal/cli/transflow/runner_test.go` - Add healing workflow test cases
   277→
   278→**Job Template Integration:**
   279→- Use existing: `roadmap/transflow/jobs/*.hcl` - Job templates with placeholder substitution
   280→- Workspace structure: `${workspace}/planner/`, `${workspace}/llm-exec/${option-id}/`, etc.
   281→
   282→**Volume Mount Points:**
   283→- Host volumes require Nomad cluster configuration
   284→- Local development: Workspace subdirectories mounted as host volumes
   285→- Production: Shared storage backend (SeaweedFS) integration
   286→
   287→#### Key Integration Points
   288→
   289→**With Existing Systems:**
   290→1. **orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()** - Already implemented, needs integration
   291→2. **TransflowRunner.ApplyDiffAndBuild()** - Already handles diff validation and build gates
   292→3. **TransflowRunner.RenderPlannerAssets()** - Asset preparation (partially implemented)
   293→4. **SharedPush build checking** - Build gate validation after diff application
   294→
   295→**Error Handling Patterns:**
   296→- Follow existing patterns in `api/arf/openrewrite_dispatcher.go`
   297→- Timeout handling consistent with `internal/orchestration/submit.go`
   298→- Job cancellation via context and channel patterns (fanout sketch)
   299→- Artifact validation before declaring success
   300→
   301→## Work Log
   302→
   303→- [2025-01-09] Created task, ready to begin implementation following TDD cycle
   304→- [2025-01-09] **COMPLETED** - Full TDD implementation of job submission infrastructure:
   305→

   307→

    79→        "content": "     1→package transflow
     2→
     3→import (

    14→
    15→// GitOperationsInterface defines the Git operations needed by the runner
    16→type GitOperationsInterface interface {
    17→	CloneRepository(ctx context.Context, repoURL, branch, targetPath string) error
    18→	CreateBranchAndCheckout(ctx context.Context, repoPath, branchName string) error
    19→	CommitChanges(ctx context.Context, repoPath, message string) error
    20→	PushBranch(ctx context.Context, repoPath, remoteURL, branchName string) error
    21→}
    22→
    23→// RecipeExecutorInterface defines the recipe execution interface
    24→type RecipeExecutorInterface interface {
    25→	ExecuteRecipes(ctx context.Context, workspacePath string, recipeIDs []string) error
    26→}
    27→
    28→// BuildCheckerInterface defines the build check interface
    29→type BuildCheckerInterface interface {
    30→	CheckBuild(ctx context.Context, config common.DeployConfig) (*common.DeployResult, error)
    31→}
    32→
    33→// StepResult represents the result of executing a single step
    34→type StepResult struct {
    35→	StepID   string
    36→	Success  bool
    37→	Message  string
    38→	Duration time.Duration
    39→}
    40→
    41→// TransflowResult represents the overall result of a transflow execution
    42→type TransflowResult struct {

    45→	BranchName     string
    46→	CommitSHA      string
    47→	BuildVersion   string
    48→	StepResults    []StepResult
    49→	ErrorMessage   string
    50→	Duration       time.Duration

    21→
    22→// RunHealingFanout executes parallel healing branches with first-success-wins semantics
    23→func (o *fanoutOrchestrator) RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error) {
    24→	if len(branches) == 0 {
    25→		return BranchResult{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("no branches to execute")
    26→	}
    27→
    28→	// Create context for cancellation when first branch succeeds
    29→	fanoutCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
    30→	defer cancel()
    31→
    32→	// Channel to receive results
    33→	resultCh := make(chan BranchResult, len(branches))
    34→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    35→
    36→	// Semaphore to limit parallelism
    37→	sem := make(chan struct{}, maxParallel)
    38→
    39→	// Launch all branches
    40→	for _, branch := range branches {
    41→		wg.Add(1)
    42→		go func(b BranchSpec) {
    43→			defer wg.Done()
    44→
    45→			// Acquire semaphore slot
    46→			select {
    47→			case sem <- struct{}{}:
    48→			case <-fanoutCtx.Done():
    49→				// Context cancelled, branch doesn't execute
    50→				resultCh <- BranchResult{

     2→
     3→## [2025-09-05] - Transflow: Complete CLI Integration MVP
     4→
     5→### Added
     6→- **Complete End-to-End CLI Integration**: Full implementation of `ploy transflow run` command with comprehensive workflow execution.
     7→  - Recipe execution with proper executable path resolution and ARF integration reuse.
     8→  - Git operations with automatic configuration (user.name/user.email) for commit operations.
     9→  - Build validation using existing SharedPush infrastructure with configurable timeouts.
    10→  - Branch management with deterministic naming and push operations to remote repositories.
    11→  - GitLab MR integration with full create/update functionality and environment variable configuration.
    12→- **Comprehensive CLI Documentation**: Complete usage guide with examples and configuration patterns.
    13→  - Basic workflow examples for Java 11→17 migration and multi-step transformations.
    14→  - Advanced execution modes including specialized healing workflows and debugging options.
    15→  - Environment configuration guide with required and optional variables.
    16→  - Expected output examples for successful workflows and self-healing scenarios.
    17→  - Integration guide showing how transflow CLI leverages existing Ploy infrastructure.
    18→- **Test Mode Infrastructure**: Complete mock implementation framework for CI/CD and local testing.
    19→  - `TestModeBuildChecker`: Mock build validation without external controller dependencies.
    20→  - `MockGitProvider`: GitLab provider simulation for testing without API calls.
    21→  - `--test-mode` CLI flag: Enable full workflow testing with mock implementations.
    22→- **Real Test Repository**: Created `ploy-orw-java11-maven` Java 11 Maven project for realistic testing.
    23→  - Java 11 codebase with OpenRewrite-transformable patterns (string operations, Optional usage, stream processing).
    24→  - Pre-configured OpenRewrite Maven plugin for Java 11→17 migration testing.
    25→  - Published to GitLab (https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git) for integration test scenarios.
    26→  - Replaces fake repository URLs in all test configurations with real, working repository.
    27→- **Integration Test Framework**: Comprehensive test suite covering end-to-end workflow scenarios.
    28→  - Configuration validation with timeout parsing and required field checks.
    29→  - Factory pattern tests for production vs. test mode implementations.
    30→  - End-to-end integration tests with proper timeout handling and workspace management.
    31→- **Enhanced Error Handling**: Robust error management throughout the workflow pipeline.
    32→  - Git commit handling with "nothing to commit" detection and graceful handling.
    33→  - Build timeout detection and proper error reporting.
    34→  - Recipe execution with fallback error handling and detailed error messages.
    35→
    36→### Enhanced  
    37→- **CLI Argument Parsing**: Complete flag support for all transflow operations including test mode, dry run, and verbose output.
    38→- **Configuration Validation**: Extended validation including build timeout format checking and comprehensive error reporting.
    39→- **Workspace Management**: Temporary directory handling with proper cleanup and configurable work directories.
    40→- **Dependency Injection**: Clean factory pattern for creating production vs. test implementations of all interfaces.
    41→
    42→### Fixed
    43→- **Recipe Executor Path Resolution**: Fixed executable discovery to use current process path instead of PATH lookup.
    44→- **Git Commit Configuration**: Automatic git user configuration for environments without global git setup.  
    45→- **Build Checker Timeout**: Implemented mock build checker to avoid hanging on unavailable controllers.
    46→- **Self-Healing Test Infrastructure**: Fixed failing self-healing tests by using real repository URLs and proper success tracking.
    47→  - Updated all test configurations to use real GitLab repository instead of fake URLs.
    48→  - Fixed missing `SetFinalResult(true)` call in healing success workflow path.
    49→  - Resolved integration test timeouts with unified mock implementation framework.
    50→- **Error Message Consistency**: Standardized error formatting and context propagation throughout the workflow.
    51→
    52→### Technical Details
    53→- **Architecture**: Complete dependency injection with interface-based design enabling testability and extensibility.
    54→- **Test Coverage**: Unit tests, integration tests, and configuration validation tests with proper mocking.
    55→- **CLI Integration**: Full command-line interface with help text, flag parsing, and error handling.
    56→- **Workflow Engine**: Complete step-by-step execution with result tracking and progress reporting.
    57→
    58→### Notes
    59→- **MVP Status**: Core transflow CLI integration is now complete and fully functional for basic recipe application workflows.
    60→- **Ready for Healing**: Infrastructure prepared for LangGraph healing integration (Stream 2 continuation).
    61→- **Production Ready**: Full test mode support enables CI/CD integration and local development workflows.
    62→- **Performance**: Efficient workspace management and parallel-capable architecture for future scaling.
    63→
    64→## [2025-09-05] - Transflow: GitLab Merge Request Integration (Stream 3, Phase 1)
    65→
    66→### Added
    67→- **GitLab MR Integration**: Complete GitLab merge request creation and update functionality for transflow workflows.
    68→  - `internal/git/provider`: New provider package with GitLab REST API client supporting project inference from HTTPS URLs.
    69→  - `GitProvider` interface: Clean abstraction supporting multiple git forge providers (GitLab now, GitHub future).
    70→  - `CreateOrUpdateMR`: GitLab API integration with automatic MR creation/updates using deterministic branch names.
    71→  - Environment-based authentication: `GITLAB_URL` and `GITLAB_TOKEN` configuration following established patterns.
    72→- **TransflowRunner Integration**: Seamless MR creation after successful workflow builds.
    73→  - Optional MR step: Failures don't break the workflow, successful builds can still complete without MRs.
    74→  - Rich MR descriptions: Automatic generation with workflow details, applied transformations, and self-healing summaries.
    75→  - Default labeling: Consistent `ploy` and `tfl` labels for workflow identification and filtering.
    76→- **Test Coverage**: Comprehensive unit and integration tests with mocked GitLab API responses.
    77→  - Unit tests: GitLab API client, URL parsing, configuration validation, error handling.
    78→  - Integration tests: End-to-end transflow workflow with GitLab provider integration.
    79→  - TDD implementation: Full RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle with proper build validation.
    80→
    81→### Enhanced
    82→- **TransflowResult**: Extended with `MRURL` field to capture created merge request URLs in workflow results.
    83→- **Result Summary**: Updated to include merge request URLs in human-readable workflow summaries.
    84→- **Branch Reuse**: Deterministic branch naming (`workflow/{id}/{timestamp}`) enables MR updates on subsequent runs.
    85→- **Error Resilience**: MR creation failures are logged but don't fail the entire workflow, maintaining robustness.
    86→
    87→### Technical Details
    88→- **Provider Pattern**: Follows existing DNS provider abstraction for clean multi-provider support.
    89→- **GitLab API**: RESTful integration using standard HTTP client with proper error handling and response parsing.
    90→- **URL Inference**: Smart project extraction from HTTPS repository URLs supporting nested namespaces.
    91→- **Configuration**: Zero-config defaults (GitLab SaaS) with environment variable overrides for self-hosted instances.
    92→- **Integration Points**: Clean dependency injection in `TransflowRunner` with proper interface boundaries.
    93→
    94→### Notes
    95→- Completes Stream 3, Phase 1 requirements from transflow MVP roadmap.
    96→- Ready for GitHub provider implementation (Stream 3, Phase 2) using same interface pattern.
    97→- MR integration is optional and gracefully degrades when GitLab is unavailable or misconfigured.
    98→- Supports both GitLab SaaS and self-hosted instances through `GITLAB_URL` configuration.
    99→
   100→## [2025-01-09] - Transflow: Job Submission Infrastructure Implementation (MVP Complete)

     8→// Job submission types for transflow healing workflow

    10→// JobSpec describes a job to be submitted to the orchestrator
    11→type JobSpec struct {

    23→	Status    string                 `json:"status"` // "completed", "failed", "timeout"
    24→	Output    string                 `json:"output"`
    25→	Error     string                 `json:"error"`
    26→	Duration  time.Duration          `json:"duration"`

    35→

    46→	Inputs map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
    47→}
    48→

   240→        "content": "     1→## MVP: transflow (Minimal Viable Implementation)
     2→
     3→Purpose: deliver a basic, working transflow that can apply OpenRewrite recipes, heal build errors via LangGraph planner/reducer jobs with parallel options, and open a GitLab MR.
     4→
     5→## Implementation Status
     6→
     7→✅ **Fully Implemented:**
     8→- OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration
     9→- Build check via `/v1/apps/:app/builds` (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    10→- YAML configuration parsing and validation
    11→- Git operations (clone, branch, commit, push)
    12→- Diff validation and application utilities
    13→- GitLab MR integration with environment variable configuration
    14→- Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`) with full end-to-end workflow
    15→- Test mode infrastructure with mock implementations for CI/testing

    17→⚠️ **Partially Implemented:**

    29→  - Reuse existing ARF/OpenRewrite pipeline (services/openrewrite-jvm, API/CLI `ploy arf transform`).
    30→  - Input: repo URL + branch, ordered recipe IDs. Output: local patch/commit applied to a workflow branch.

    32→ - LLM plan and exec

    34→  - `llm-plan` (Stream 2/Phase 2): produce a list of `llm-exec` steps (sequential only in MVP) from repo state + last error.

    39→  - Use existing build API to verify the code builds without deploying.

    42→  - Success criteria: HTTP 200; response includes `lane`, `image` or `dockerImage`. For logs, optional `GET /v1/apps/:app/logs`.

    44→  - App naming: derive unique app name `tfw-<transflow-id>-<timestamp>` unless explicitly provided.

    46→  - Timeout: request timeout configurable per run; default 10m for build check.

    49→  - Create a workflow branch at start; commit changes from steps; push branch to origin using provided token envs.
    50→  - GitLab MR: infer project from `target_repo`, target branch is `base_ref`; envs `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN`.

   264→              "old_string": "# Transflow Service CLAUDE.md

## Purpose
Orchestrates multi-step code transformation workflows with automated build validation and GitLab merge request integration.

## Narrative Summary
The transflow service provides a complete pipeline for applying code transformations (via OpenRewrite recipes) to target repositories, validating the results through automated builds, and optionally creating GitLab merge requests for review. The service supports self-healing workflows that can automatically retry failed transformations with alternative strategies.

Core workflow: clone repository → create branch → apply transformations → commit changes → validate build → create/update merge request. The service integrates with existing ARF (Application Recipe Framework) infrastructure for recipe execution and SharedPush for build validation.",
   265→              "new_string": "# Transflow CLI Module CLAUDE.md

## Purpose
Complete CLI integration for orchestrating multi-step code transformation workflows with automated build validation, self-healing capabilities, and GitLab merge request integration.

## Narrative Summary
The transflow module provides end-to-end implementation of `ploy transflow run` command supporting complete transformation pipelines. It applies code transformations via OpenRewrite recipes, validates results through automated builds, creates GitLab merge requests for review, and includes sophisticated self-healing workflows with LangGraph-based job orchestration.

Core workflow: clone repository → create branch → apply transformations → commit changes → validate build → create/update merge request → optional healing on failures. The module integrates with ARF infrastructure for recipe execution, SharedPush for build validation, and includes comprehensive job submission infrastructure for healing workflows."

- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS` - Tool configuration JSON for healing jobs
- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS` - Execution limits JSON for healing jobs
- `NOMAD_ADDR` - Nomad cluster address for job submission

CLI flags:
- `--config, -f` - Configuration file path
- `--test-mode` - Enable mock implementations for testing
- `--dry-run` - Show what would be done without execution
- `--verbose` - Detailed output during workflow execution"
